author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew X. 23.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into the other; for verily I say unto you, ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of Man be come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having spoken of those fearful and horrible things, enough to melt very adamant, which after His cross, and resurrection, and assumption, were to befall them, He directs again His discourse to what was of more tranquil character, allowing those whom He is training to recover breath, and affording them full security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He did not at all command them, when persecuted, to close with the enemy, but to fly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, it being so far but a beginning, and a prelude, He gave His discourse a very condescending turn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not now of the ensuing persecutions is He speaking, but of those before the cross and the passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He showed by saying, “Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of Man be come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, lest they should say, “What then, if when persecuted we flee, and there again they overtake us, and drive us out?”—to destroy this fear, He saith, “Ye shall not have gone round Palestine first, but I will straightway come upon you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how here again He doeth not away with the terrors, but stands by them in their perils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “I will snatch you out, and will put an end to the persecutions;” but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of Man be come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for it sufficed for their consolation, simply to see Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou observe, I pray thee, how He doth not on every occasion leave all to grace, but requires something also to be contributed on their part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if ye fear,” saith He, “flee,” for this He signified by saying, “flee ye,” and “fear not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He did not command them to flee at first, but when persecuted to withdraw; neither is it a great distance that He allows them, but so much as to go about the cities of Israel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then again, He trains them for another branch of self-command; first, casting out all care for their food: secondly, all fear of their perils; and now, that of calumny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since from that first anxiety He freed them, by saying, “The workman is worthy of his hire,”and by signifying that many would receive them; and from their distress about their dangers, by saying, “Take no thought how or what ye shall speak,” and, “He that endureth unto the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since withal it was likely that they should also bring upon themselves an evil report, which to many seems harder to bear than all; see whence He comforts them even in this case, deriving the encouragement from Himself, and from all that had been said touching Himself; to which nothing else was equal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as He said in that other place, “Ye shall be hated of all men,” and added, “for my name’s sake,” so also here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in another way He mitigates it, joining a fresh topic to that former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What kind of one then is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “The disciple,” saith He, “is not above his Master, nor the servant above his Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his Master, and the servant as his Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If they have called the Master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of His household?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fear them not therefore.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how He discovers Himself to be the Lord and God and Creator of all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is there not any disciple above his Master, or servant above his Lord?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So long as he is a disciple, and a servant, he is not, by the nature of that honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me not here of the rare instances, but take the principle from the majority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He saith not, “How much more His servants,” but “them of His household,” to show how very near He felt them to be to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And elsewhere too He said, “Henceforth I call you not servants; ye are my friends.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not, If they have insulted the Master of the house, and calumniated Him; but states also the very form of the insult, that they “called Him Beelzebub.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He gives also another consolation, not inferior to this: for this indeed is the greatest; but because for them who were not yet living strictly, there was need also of another, such as might have special power to refresh them, He states it likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the saying seems indeed in form to be an universal proposition, nevertheless not of all matters, but of those in hand only, is it spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “There is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; nor hid, that shall not be known.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now what He saith is like this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is indeed sufficient for your encouragement, that I also shared with you in the same reproach; I who am your Master and Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if it still grieve you to hear these words, consider this other thing too, that even from this suspicion ye will soon be released.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For why do ye grieve?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At their calling you sorcerers and deceivers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wait a little, and all men will address you as saviors, and benefactors of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for time discovers all things that are concealed, it will both refute their false accusation, and make manifest your virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when the event shows you saviors, and benefactors, and examples of all virtue, men will not give heed to their words, but to the real state of the case; and they will appear false accusers, and liars, and slanderers, but ye brighter than the sun, length of time revealing and proclaiming you, and uttering a voice clearer than a trumpet, and making all men witnesses of your virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let not therefore what is now said humble you, but let the hope of the good things to come raise you up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it cannot be, that what relates to you should be hid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, having rid them of all distress, and fears, and anxiety, and set them above men’s reproaches, then, and not till then, He seasonably discourses to them also of boldness in their preaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “What I tell you,” saith He, “in darkness, that speak ye in light; and what ye have heard in the ear, that preach yeupon the housetops.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet it was not at all darkness, when He was saying these things; neither was He dis coursing unto them in the ear; but He used a strong figure, thus speaking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, because He was conversing with them alone, and in a small corner of Palestine, therefore He said, “in darkness,” and “in the ear;” contrasting the boldness of speech, which He was hereafter to confer on them, with the tone of the conversation which was then going on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For not to one, or two, or three cities, but to the whole world ye shall preach,” saith He, “traversing land and sea, the inhabited country, and the desert; to princes alike and tribes, to philosophers and orators, saying all with open face,and with all boldness of speech.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, He said, “On the house tops,” and, “In the light,” without any shrinking, and with all freedom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore said He not only, “Preach on the housetops,” and “Speak in the light,” but added also, “What I tell you in darkness,” and “What ye hear in the ear”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was to raise up their spirits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As therefore when He said, “He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also, and greater works than these shall he do;”even so here too, to signify that He will do it all by them, and more than by Himself, He inserted this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “the beginning indeed,” saith He, “I have given, and the prelude; but the greater part it is my will to effect through you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now this is the language of one not commanding only, but also declaring beforehand what was to be, and encouraging them with His sayings, and implying that they should prevail over all, and quietly also removingagain their distress at the evil report.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as this doctrine, after lying hid for a while, shall overspread all things, so also the evil suspicion of the Jews shall quickly perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, because He had lifted them up on high, He again gives warning of the perils also, adding wings to their mind, and exalting them high above all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He set them far above all things, persuading them to despise not anxiety only and calumny, dangers and plots, but even that which is esteemed of all things most terrible, death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not death alone, but by violence too?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not, “ye shall be slain,” but with the dignity that became Him, He set this before them, saying, “Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul; but rather fear Himwhich is able to destroy both soul and body in hell;” bringing round the argument, as He ever doth, to its opposite.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what? is your fear, saith He, of death? and are ye therefore slow to preach?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay for this very cause I bid you preach, that ye fear death: for this shall deliver you from that which is really death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What though they shall slay you? yet over the better part they shall not prevail, though they strive ten thousand ways.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said not, “Who do not kill the soul,” but, who “are not able to kill.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wish it as they may, they shall not prevail.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, if thou fear punishment, fear that, the more grievous by far.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how again He doth not promise them deliverance from death, but permits them to die, granting them more than if He had not allowed them to suffer it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because deliverance from death is not near so great as persuading men to despise death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: You see now, He doth not push them into dangers, but sets them above dangers, and in a short sentence fixes in their mind the doctrines that relate to the immortality of the soul, and having in two or three words implanted a saving doctrine, He comforts them also by other considerations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, lest they should think, when killed and butchered, that as men forsaken they suffered this, He introduces again the argument of God’s providence, saying on this wise: “Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And one of them shall not fall into a snarewithout your Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.”“For what is viler than they?” saith He; “nevertheless, not even these shall be taken without God’s knowledge.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He means not this, “by His operation they fall,” for this were unworthy of God; but, “nothing that is done is hid from Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then He is not ignorant of anything that befalls us, and loves us more truly than a father, and so loves us, as to have numbered our very hairs; we ought not to be afraid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He said, not that God numbers our hairs, but that He might indicate His perfect knowledge, and His great providence over them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If therefore He both knows all the things that are done, and is able to save you, and willing; whatever ye may have to suffer, think not that as persons forsaken ye suffer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is it His will to deliver you from the terrors, but to persuade you to despise them, since this is, more than anything, deliverance from the terrors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Fear ye not therefore; ye are of more value than many sparrows.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that the fear had already prevailed over them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for He knew the secrets of the heart; therefore He added, “Fear them not therefore;” for even should they prevail, it will be over the inferior part, I mean, the body; which though they should not kill, nature will surely take with her and depart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that not even this depends on them, but men have it from nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou fear this, much more shouldest thou fear what is greater, and dread “Him who is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He saith not openly now, that it is Himself, “Who is able to destroy both soul and body,” but where He before declared Himself to be judge, He made it manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now the contrary takes place: Him, namely, who is able to destroy the soul, that is, to punish it, we fear not, but those who slay the body, we shudder at.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely while He together with the soul punishes the body also, they cannot even chasten the body, much less the soul: and though they chasten it ever so severely, yet in that way they rather make it more glorious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He signifies the conflicts to be easy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because in truth, death did exceedingly agitate their souls, inspiring terror for a time, for that it had not as yet been made easy to overcome, neither had they that were to despise it partaken of the grace of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having, you see, cast out the fear and distress that was agitating their soul; by what follows He also encourages them again, casting out fear by fear; and not by fear only, but also by the hope of great prizes; and He threatens with much authority, in both ways urging them to speak boldly for the truth; and saith further,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, himwill I also confess before my Father which is in Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus not from the good things only, but also from the opposites, doth He urge them; and He concludes with the dismal part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark His exact care; He said not “me,” but “in me,” implying that not by a power of his own, but by the help of grace from above, the confessor makes his confession.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But of him that denies, He said not, “in me,” but “me;” for he having become destitute of the gift, his denial ensues.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Why then is he blamed,” one may say, “if being forsaken, he denies?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because the being forsaken is the fault of the forsaken person himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But why is He not satisfied with the faith in the mind, but requires also the confession with the mouth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To train us up to boldness in speech, and a more abundant love and determination, and to raise us on high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He addresses Himself to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nor doth He at all apply this to the disciples only in person, for not them, but their disciples too, He is now rendering noble hearted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because he that hath learnt this lesson will not only teach with boldness, but will likewise suffer all things easily, and with ready mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This at any rate brought over many to the apostles, even their belief in this word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because both in the punishment the infliction is heavier, and in the good things the recompense greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, whereas he that doeth right hath the advantage in time,and the delay of the penalty is counted for gain by the sinner: He hath introduced an equivalent, or rather a much greater advantage, the increase of the recompenses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Hast thou the advantage,” saith He, “by having first confessed me here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I also will have the advantage of thee, by giving thee greater things, and unspeakably greater; for I will confess thee there.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that both the good things and the evil things are there to be dispensed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then hasten and hurry thyself? and why seek thy rewards here, thou who art “saved by hope?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, whether thou hast done anything good, and not received its recompense here, be not troubled (for with increase, in the time to come, the reward thereof awaits thee): or whether thou hast done any evil, and not paid the penalty, be not easy; for there will vengeance receive thee, if thou turn not and amend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou believe it not, from the things here form thy conjecture about things to come also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if in the season of the conflicts they that confess are so glorious, imagine what they will be in the season of the crowns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If the enemies here applaud, how shall that tenderest of all fathers fail to admire and proclaim thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, then shall we have both our gifts for the good, and our punishments for the evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that such as deny shall suffer harm, both here and there; here living with an evil conscience, though they were never to die, they shall be surely dead; and there, undergoing the last penalty: but the other sort will profit both here and there, both here making a gain of their death, and in this way becoming more glorious than the living, and there enjoying those unspeakable blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: God then is in no wise prompt to punish only, but also to confer benefits; and for this last more than for the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But why hath He put the reward once only, the punishment twice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He knows that this would be more apt to correct us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause when He had said, “Fear Him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell,” He saith again, “Him will I also deny.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So doth Paul also, continually making mention of hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus we see that He, having by all ways trained on His scholar (both by opening Heaven to him, and by setting before him that fearful judgment-seat, and by pointing to the amphitheatre of angels, and how in the midst of them the crowns shall be proclaimed, which thing would thenceforth prepare the way for the word of godliness to be very easily received); in what follows, lest they grow timid and the word be hindered, He bids them be prepared even for slaughter itself; to make them aware that such as continue in their error, will have to suffer (among other things) for plotting against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us therefore despise death, although the time be not come that requires it of us; for indeed it will translate us to a far better life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But the body decays.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, on this account most especially we ought to rejoice, because death decays, and mortality perishes, not the substance of the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither, shouldest thou see a statue being cast, wouldest thou call the process destruction, but an improved formation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Just so do thou reason also concerning the body, and do not bewail.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then it were right to bewail, had it remained in its chastisement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But,” saith one, “this ought to take place without the decay of our bodies; they should continue entire.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what would this have advantaged either the living or the departed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How long are ye lovers of the body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How long are ye rivetted to the earth and gaping after shadows?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, what good would this have done? or rather, what harm would it not have done?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For did our bodies not decay, in the first place the greatest of all evils, pride, would have continued with many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even while this is going on, and worms gushing out, many have earnestly sought to be gods; what would not have been the result did the body continue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the second place, it would not be believed to be of earth; for if, its end witnessing this, some yet doubt; what would they not have suspected if they did not see this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thirdly, the bodies would have been excessively loved; and most men would have become more carnal and gross; and if even now some cleave to men’s tombs and coffins, after that themselves have perished, what would they not have done, if they had even their image preserved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fourthly, they would not have earnestly desired the things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fifthly, they that say the world is eternal, would have been more confirmed, and would have denied God as Creator.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Sixthly, they would not have known the excellence of the soul, and how great a thing is the presence of a soul in a body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seventhly, many of them that lose their relations would have left their cities, and have dwelt in the tombs, and have become frantic, conversing continually with their own dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even now men form to themselves images, since they cannot keep the body (for neither is it possible, but whether they will or no it glides and hurries from them), and are rivetted to the planks of wood; what monstrous thing would they not then have devised?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To my thinking, the generality would have even built temples for such bodies, and they that are skilled in such sorceries would have persuaded evil spirits to speak through them; since at least even now, they that venture on the arts of necromancy attempt many things more out of the way than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how many idolatries would not have arisen from hence? when men even after the dust and ashes, are yet eager in those practices.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: God therefore, to take away all our extravagances, and to teach us to stand off from all earthly things, destroys the bodies before our eyes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even he that is enamored of bodies, and is greatly affected at the sight of a beautiful damsel, if he will not learn by discourse the deformity of that substance, shall know it by the very sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, many of the like age with her whom he loves, and oftentimes also fairer, being dead, after the first or second day, have emitted an ill savor, and foul matter, and decay with worms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Imagine then what sort of beauty thou lovest, and what sort of elegance has power so to disturb thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if bodies did not decay, this would not be well known: but as evil spirits run unto men’s graves, so also many of our lovers, continually sitting by the tombs, would have received evil spirits in their soul, and would quickly have perished in this grievous madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as it is, together with all other things this also comforts the soul, that the form is not seen: it brings men to forgetfulness of their affliction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Indeed, if this were not so, there would be no tombs at all, but thou wouldest see our cities having corpses instead of statues, each man desiring to look upon his own dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And much confusion would arise hence, and none of the ordinary sort would attend to his soul, nor would give room to the doctrine of immortality to enter in: and many other things too, more shocking than these, would have resulted, which even to speak of were unseemly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore it decays presently, that thou mightest see unveiled the beauty of the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if she be the procurer of all that beauty and life, much more excellent must she herself be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if she preserve that which is so deformed and unsightly, much more herself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not the body wherein the beauty lies, but the expression,and the bloom which is shed over its substance by the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now then, I bid thee love that which makes the body also to appear such as it is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why speak I of death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay even in life itself, I would have thee mark how all is hers that is beautiful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whether she be pleased, she showers roses over the cheeks; or whether she be pained, she takes that beauty, and involves it all in a dark robe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if she be continually in mirth, the body improves in condition; if in grief, she renders the same thinner and weaker than a spider’s web; if in wrath, she hath made it again abominable and foul; if she show the eye calm, great is the beauty that she bestows; if she express envy, very pale and livid is the hue she sheds over us; if love, abundant the gracefulness she at once confers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus in fact many women, not being beautiful in feature, have derived much grace from the soul; others again of brilliant bloom, by having an ungracious soul, have marred their beauty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider how a face that is pale grows red, and by the variation of color produces great delight, when there is need of shame and blushing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As, on the other hand, if it be shameless, it makes the countenance more unpleasing than any monster.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing is fairer, nothing sweeter than a beauteous soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while as to bodies, the longing is with pain, in the case of souls the pleasure is pure and calm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then let go the king, and be wild about the herald?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why leave the philosopher, and gape after his interpreter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou seen a beautiful eye? acquaint thyself with that which is within; and if that be not beautiful, despise this likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For surely, didst thou see an ill-favored woman wearing a beautiful mask, she would make no impression on thee: just as on the other hand, neither wouldest thou suffer one fair and beautiful to be disguised by the mask, but wouldest take it away, as choosing to see her beauty unveiled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then I bid thee do in regard of the soul also, and acquaint thyself with it first; for this is clad with the body instead of a mask; wherefore also that abides such as it is; but the other, though it be mishapen, may quickly become beautiful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though it have an eye that is unsightly, and harsh, and fierce, it may become beautiful, mild, calm, sweet-tempered, gentle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This beauty therefore let us seek, this countenance let us adorn; that God also may “have pleasure in our beauty,”and impart to us of His everlasting blessings, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
